findings.evidence: Line 61 exposes `lemma symmetricPart_isSymm (A : _root_.Matrix n n ℝ) : (symmetricPart A).IsSymm`; nearby normal forms are annotated, e.g. lines 42, 49, and 74 have `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `symmetricPart_isSymm` (or the appropriate `@[grind]` annotation if the intended downstream automation is `grind`).
findings.issue: `symmetricPart_isSymm` is the characteristic introduction rule for `TauCeti.Matrix.symmetricPart`, but it is not annotated for automation, so users proving later self-adjoint/symmetric-coefficient goals must unfold or name it manually.

summary: The transpose and ellipticity-stability surface is in scope, but one characteristic symmetric-part lemma is exposed without the automation annotation needed for normal use.
